sellWithUs.title: Bring your store to shoppers across Jordan
sellWithUs.description: Tell us who you are and our team will contact you to launch your catalog, set up payments, and get your storefront live.

features.fastDescription: Share your core details now and we will guide you through the next steps.
features.followUpDescription: A member of our merchant team will contact you to confirm your business info.
features.supportDescription: We help you prepare listings, pricing, and storefront visibility for launch.

sellWithUs.privacy: Your details are only used to contact you about opening your store on this marketplace.
sellWithUs.formDescription: Fill in these details and we will get back to you shortly.

validation.phone: Please enter a valid phone number.

sellWithUs.submitError: We couldn't submit your request right now. Please try again.
sellWithUs.footnote: By submitting this form, you agree that our team may contact you by phone about store onboarding.
sellWithUs.successTitle: Your request is on its way
sellWithUs.successDescription: Thanks. Our merchant team will review your details and contact you soon.

newsletter.successDesc: You'll receive our latest updates and exclusive offers.
newsletter.title: Stay Updated with Our Newsletter
newsletter.desc: Subscribe to get exclusive offers, early access to sales, and new product updates.
newsletter.disclaimer: By subscribing, you agree to our Privacy Policy and consent to receive updates.

home.heroSubtitle: Discover amazing products at great prices
home.exploreCategories: Explore our wide range of categories
home.featuredSubtitle: Our handpicked selection of premium items
home.newsletterSubtitle: Get updates on new products and exclusive offers

checkout.numbersOnly: Use Arabic or English digits only

footer.description: Your premier destination for online shopping - Quality products, great prices, fast delivery
footer.subscribeDesc: Get updates on new products and exclusive offers
footer.newsletterInvalidEmail: Enter a valid email address to join the newsletter preview.

promo.summerCollectionDesc: Up to 40% off on selected items

topBar.announcement: 🎉 Free shipping on orders over {amount} JD!

categories.errorLoading: Error loading categories. Please try again.

categories.noProducts: No products found in this category yet.

search.startSearchingDesc: Enter a search term to find products, brands, and more
search.noProductsMatch: No products match your search criteria.
search.noResultsHint: Try a different search or remove some filters

notFound.title: Page Not Found
notFound.description: The page you are looking for might have been removed, had its name changed, or is temporarily unavailable.

sale.title: Sale
sale.subtitle: Browse current discounts in one focused place without losing the storefront shopping flow.
sale.intro: This page surfaces products that already have real discounted pricing so shoppers can move faster when they are looking for value.

realDeals.title: Real discounted products
realDeals.description: Only products with an actual lower sale price appear here, so the page stays useful instead of becoming a generic campaign banner.

freshDrops.title: Fresh markdowns
freshDrops.description: Newly discounted items can rise quickly, which makes this page a strong first stop for price-sensitive browsing.

trustedPricing.title: Clear price context
trustedPricing.description: The storefront keeps the current price, previous price, and savings visible so the offer stays easy to understand.

howItWorks.title: How the sale page works
howItWorks.body: The page stays front-end only while still using the existing product data already available in the storefront.

items.livePricing: Products are filtered from the existing catalog by real sale pricing.
items.visibleSavings: Cards keep the savings treatment shoppers expect from a sale destination.
items.fastRotation: The grid can refresh naturally as catalog pricing changes over time.

smartShopping.title: Smart ways to shop the page
smartShopping.body: Use the sale page as a shortcut, then jump back to the broader catalog whenever you want to continue browsing.

items.compare: Check the original and discounted prices before you add to cart.
items.saveFavorites: Use wishlist and cart actions exactly as you do on the rest of the storefront.
items.checkout: Move from the sale page into the normal checkout journey without a separate flow.

cta.title: Want to keep browsing beyond discounted items?
cta.description: Jump back to the main catalog for the full assortment or contact the team if you need help finding a specific offer.

sale.emptyDescription: No discounted products are available in the current front-end view right now. Please check back soon or browse the full catalog.

giftCards.title: Gift Cards
giftCards.subtitle: A polished gift-card landing page that explains the value clearly, even before a full purchase backend is connected.
giftCards.intro: Use gift cards when you want the experience to feel thoughtful and flexible at the same time.

flexible.title: Flexible gifting
flexible.description: Let recipients choose what they actually want instead of forcing a one-size-fits-all purchase.

personal.title: Personal and practical
personal.description: Gift cards work for birthdays, thank-yous, celebrations, and quick last-minute gifting.

easyToUse.title: Easy to redeem
easyToUse.description: The page is framed so the future checkout connection can be added later without redesigning the customer experience.

howItWorks.title: How it works
howItWorks.body: The page presents the gift-card flow clearly today while staying honest that fulfillment can be wired in later.

items.chooseAmount: Choose the amount that matches the occasion.
items.sendDetails: Share recipient details and gifting notes with the support team.
items.redeemLater: Recipients can use the value later when they are ready to shop.

whyPeopleLoveIt.title: Why shoppers choose gift cards
whyPeopleLoveIt.body: Gift cards reduce friction and still feel thoughtful when the product choice should stay with the recipient.

items.birthdays: Useful for birthdays and family celebrations.
items.corporate: Great for employee rewards and partner thank-yous.
items.lastMinute: Ideal for last-minute gifting without compromising presentation.

cta.title: Need help arranging a gift-card order?
cta.description: Reach out to the team and we can guide you through the current frontend-only setup.

contact.title: Contact Us
contact.subtitle: We're here to help. Reach out anytime.

form.title: Send a message
form.description: Fill out the form and we'll get back to you as soon as possible.
form.successDescription: Thank you for reaching out. We'll respond shortly.

faqs.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faqs.subtitle: Grouped answers for the questions shoppers usually ask before, during, and after checkout.
faqs.intro: The FAQ is organized to reduce friction and point people to the right next step when they still need help.

fastAnswers.title: Fast answers
fastAnswers.description: Clear sections make it easier to scan common questions without leaving the storefront context.

delivery.title: Delivery guidance
delivery.description: Shipping and order-progress questions are grouped so customers can self-serve first.

returns.title: Policy clarity
returns.description: Important service and delivery expectations are explained in practical language rather than dense policy text.

cta.title: Still need a hand?
cta.description: If your answer is not here, reach out directly or review the shipping page next.

shopping.title: Shopping
shopping.q1: How do I find the right product faster?
shopping.a1: Use search, category browsing, brand pages, and the listing filters together to narrow the catalog quickly.
shopping.q2: Can I save products for later?
shopping.a2: Yes. Wishlist and cart flows are available directly from product cards and product detail pages.
shopping.q3: Do product prices update on the page?
shopping.a3: The storefront reflects current catalog pricing, including valid sale pricing where applicable.

payment.title: Payment
payment.q1: Which payment options are shown during checkout?
payment.a1: The storefront presents the payment methods currently configured for the checkout flow.
payment.q2: Can I use my wallet balance when ordering?
payment.a2: If wallet functionality is available for your account, the checkout flow will surface it during payment selection.
payment.q3: Why can a payment total change before I place the order?
payment.a3: Delivery fees, promotions, and cart changes can affect the final total before confirmation.

shipping.title: Shipping
shipping.q1: How long does shipping usually take?
shipping.a1: Delivery timing depends on order confirmation, destination, and current processing volume.
shipping.q2: When do I get free shipping?
shipping.a2: Orders above the current free-shipping threshold can qualify automatically during checkout.
shipping.q3: How do I check my order status?
shipping.a3: Sign in to review your order history in the account area, or contact support if you need delivery help.

account.title: Account and orders
account.q1: Do I need an account to keep track of orders?
account.a1: An account makes order history, addresses, and saved items easier to manage in one place.
account.q2: Where can I update my saved addresses?
account.a2: Address management is available from the profile area once you are signed in.
account.q3: Where can I ask for help with an order issue?
account.a3: Use the contact page and include your order details so the support team can review the case quickly.

shipping.title: Shipping Info
shipping.subtitle: Straightforward shipping guidance built around the storefront’s existing fee and threshold rules.
shipping.intro: Standard delivery currently starts from {fee} JD, and eligible orders above {threshold} JD can unlock free shipping.

standard.title: Clear delivery expectations
standard.description: The page explains timing, fees, and the order journey in language shoppers can scan quickly.

reliable.title: Practical guidance
reliable.description: Shipping content stays grounded in the current storefront behavior rather than generic promises.

visibility.title: Easy follow-up
visibility.description: The next action is always obvious, whether that is tracking an order or contacting support.

deliveryWindows.title: Delivery windows
deliveryWindows.body: Shipping times use Amman local time. Orders confirmed before the daily cutoff move faster.

items.processing: Order by 2:00 PM Sunday–Thursday to receive your shipment tomorrow.
items.cityCoverage: Order after 2:00 PM Sunday–Wednesday and delivery is expected in 2 days.
items.busyPeriods: Orders placed Thursday after cutoff, Friday, or Saturday arrive on Sunday.

fees.title: Fees and thresholds
fees.body: The storefront currently uses a standard shipping fee of {fee} JD, while orders above {threshold} JD can qualify for free shipping.

items.finalReview: Final shipping charges are always reviewed during checkout before confirmation.

cta.title: Ready to continue shopping?
cta.description: Browse the catalog or contact the team directly if you need delivery help for an existing order.

returns.title: Returns & Exchanges
returns.subtitle: A readable returns page that explains eligibility, review, exchange steps, and refund expectations without legal overload.
returns.intro: This page is designed to set expectations clearly before a shopper reaches out about an exchange or refund.

clearPolicy.title: Clear eligibility
clearPolicy.description: Shoppers can quickly understand when a return or exchange request is likely to move forward.

fairReview.title: Fair review process
fairReview.description: Requests are framed around condition, proof, and practicality rather than ambiguous wording.

supportedSteps.title: Guided next steps
supportedSteps.description: The page makes the support path obvious when a shopper is ready to open a conversation.

eligibility.title: Eligibility and condition
eligibility.body: Returns and exchanges work best when the item condition and request timing are both clear from the start.

items.condition: Items should remain in acceptable condition for review.
items.packaging: Original packaging and included pieces help the request move faster.
items.proof: Order information and a short explanation of the issue are helpful.

refunds.title: Refunds and exchanges
refunds.body: After a request is reviewed, the next step depends on availability, item condition, and the chosen resolution.

items.review: A quick review helps confirm eligibility and the appropriate outcome.
items.exchange: If a replacement is suitable and available, exchange guidance can follow.
items.timeline: Refund timing can vary depending on the payment path and final approval.

cta.title: Need help with a return request?
cta.description: Contact the support team with your order details so the conversation starts with the right context.

trackOrder.title: Track Order
trackOrder.subtitle: A frontend-only order lookup experience that still guides customers toward the right self-service and support paths.
trackOrder.intro: Use this page to understand what stage an order is likely in, then move into your account or support if you need more help.

lookup.title: Simple lookup
lookup.description: The track-order form is presented in a familiar way so the live backend can be connected later without redesigning the page.

status.title: Status guidance
status.description: The page explains what common order stages usually mean for a shopper.

support.title: Clear escalation path
support.description: When a customer still needs help, the next support action is visible immediately.

statusGuide.title: Status guide
statusGuide.body: Understanding each stage helps shoppers know whether they need to wait, sign in, or ask for support.

items.confirmed: Confirmed means the order details were accepted and queued for fulfillment.
items.processing: Processing means preparation is still underway before delivery handoff.
items.delivered: Delivered means the order should already be at the final destination.

needHelp.title: Before you ask for help
needHelp.body: A few details make order follow-up faster and more accurate.

items.keepNumber: Keep the order number ready before you contact support.
items.signIn: Signed-in users can often check their order history first.
items.contact: If the order needs manual review, contact support with the key details.

cta.title: Prefer the account view?
cta.description: Go directly to the order-history area or contact support for a manual follow-up.

lookup.title: Track by order details
lookup.description: This frontend-only lookup confirms the interaction flow today while guiding customers toward their account or support next.
lookup.successTitle: Lookup captured in the frontend preview
lookup.successDescription: The live tracking response is not connected here yet, so the page now points you to the best next step.

about.title: About Us
about.subtitle: A credible brand page for an ecommerce business serving shoppers in Jordan with a practical, trust-building voice.
about.intro: {siteName} is framed as a modern shopping destination that values clarity, quality, and a smooth customer experience from browsing to delivery.

rooted.title: Rooted in Jordan
rooted.description: The page reflects local context, contact details, and a service mindset that feels grounded instead of generic.

trust.title: Built on trust
trust.description: Clear product presentation, fair pricing, and approachable support are part of the brand promise.

customerFirst.title: Customer-first shopping
customerFirst.description: The storefront is designed to help people discover confidently and complete orders with less friction.

story.title: Our story
story.body: The brand story focuses on making ecommerce easier to trust, easier to browse, and easier to return to.

items.curatedCatalog: A curated catalog built to feel easier to explore than a cluttered marketplace.
items.localRelevance: A local operating context that makes delivery and support feel more relevant.
items.clearJourney: A clearer journey from product discovery to checkout and follow-up.

promise.title: Our promise
promise.body: The page should reassure shoppers that {siteName} is serious about consistency, transparency, and service quality.

items.quality: Present products with enough detail to make confident choices.
items.support: Keep support reachable when shoppers need help after the click.
items.transparency: Show prices and expectations clearly, including offers and delivery context.

cta.title: Ready to explore the catalog?
cta.description: Browse products now or reach out if you want help before placing an order.

careers.title: Careers
careers.subtitle: A polished careers landing page that explains culture, growth, and role areas without pretending a jobs backend already exists.
careers.intro: The page positions {siteName} as a growing team for people who want visible impact across ecommerce, operations, and customer experience.

growth.title: Room to grow
growth.description: The careers story emphasizes learning, ownership, and meaningful contribution instead of generic corporate language.

collaboration.title: Cross-team collaboration
collaboration.description: Roles are presented as connected to catalog quality, delivery execution, and better customer experiences.

impact.title: Visible impact
impact.description: Candidates can see how their work would shape the customer journey directly.

culture.title: Culture and ways of working
culture.body: The page highlights practical teamwork, accountability, and steady improvement over exaggerated startup claims.

items.ownership: People are expected to take ownership of outcomes, not only tasks.
items.customerFocus: Customer impact stays close to daily decision-making.
items.learning: Learning and iteration are treated as part of the job, not side activities.

roles.title: Open role areas
roles.body: Without a jobs backend, the page still shows the kinds of teams and functions candidates can express interest in.

items.operations: Logistics, order operations, and partner coordination.
items.support: Customer support, account experience, and service quality.

cta.title: Interested in future opportunities?
cta.description: Use the contact route to share your background and the role area that fits you best.

blog.title: Blog
blog.subtitle: A branded editorial destination that feels useful today, even before a CMS or publishing backend exists.
blog.intro: The blog page is framed as a place for shopping guidance, brand stories, seasonal ideas, and curated inspiration.

guides.title: Practical guides
guides.description: Content is positioned around helping shoppers make better purchase decisions with less friction.

trends.title: Seasonal trends
trends.description: The page can spotlight fresh collections, gifting moments, and category-led inspiration.

community.title: Community voice
community.description: The editorial tone stays close to the customer instead of sounding like a disconnected media brand.

featured.title: Featured stories
featured.body: Static seeded stories make the page feel alive now while leaving room for a future content source.

items.story1: How to build a smarter wishlist before the next big shopping moment.
items.story2: Simple ways to compare offers without losing track of value.
items.story3: Gift ideas that stay useful when you are short on time.

expect.title: What to expect from the blog
expect.body: The page promises useful content formats instead of overcomplicating the editorial direction.

items.shoppingTips: Shopping tips that shorten decision time.
items.brandStories: Brand and category stories that add context.
items.roundups: Roundups and seasonal recommendations worth saving for later.

cta.title: Ready to shop after reading?
cta.description: Move straight into the catalog or reach out if you want help finding a product mentioned on the page.

press.title: Press
press.subtitle: A clean press page for media and partners who need a fast overview of the brand, context, and contact path.
press.intro: The press page gives journalists and collaborators a simple way to understand {siteName} without needing a separate backend or media portal.

brandStory.title: Brand overview
brandStory.description: A concise introduction helps external readers understand the business quickly.

facts.title: Useful facts
facts.description: The page can summarize audience, shopping focus, and operational context in one place.

mediaContact.title: Clear media contact
mediaContact.description: A visible next step matters more than overproduced press jargon.

overview.title: Company overview
overview.body: The page presents {siteName} as an ecommerce business focused on a smoother and more trustworthy shopping experience.

items.ecommerce: A modern ecommerce storefront built around easier discovery and checkout.
items.customerExperience: A customer experience that prioritizes clarity and responsiveness.
items.brandPartnerships: A catalog model that can grow with brands, vendors, and category depth.

mediaKit.title: Media kit direction
mediaKit.body: Until a downloadable kit exists, the page still explains what media resources can be requested.

items.logo: Brand marks and basic visual references.
items.brandSummary: Short business background and positioning notes.
items.contact: A direct support path for media follow-up.

cta.title: Need a media follow-up?
cta.description: Use the contact route and note that your request is media-related so the team can respond appropriately.

affiliate.title: Affiliate Program
affiliate.subtitle: A conversion-focused affiliate page that introduces the opportunity clearly before a dedicated partner backend exists.
affiliate.intro: The page is designed for creators, publishers, and communities who want a straightforward way to collaborate with the brand.

rewards.title: Reward-driven model
rewards.description: The page explains the value of partnership without inventing unsupported commission tooling.

partners.title: Built for different partners
partners.description: Affiliates can imagine how the program would fit creators, editorial partners, and niche communities.

simpleFlow.title: Simple signup path
simpleFlow.description: The experience remains clear today even before a formal affiliate dashboard exists.

howItWorks.title: How it works
howItWorks.body: The page keeps the program explanation easy to scan so serious partners can decide whether to reach out.

items.apply: Share your audience, channel, and partnership fit.
items.share: Promote products and storefront journeys that match your audience.
items.earn: Coordinate next steps and reward details directly with the team.

partners.title: Who the program suits best
partners.body: The positioning stays broad enough for future growth while still feeling concrete.

items.creators: Creators who recommend products with strong personal context.
items.publishers: Publishers and editorial spaces that build shopping guides or roundups.
items.communities: Communities with trusted product recommendations and engaged audiences.

cta.title: Interested in partnering?
cta.description: Reach out with your audience details and the team can discuss the current front-end-only setup.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.subtitle: A readable privacy page that explains how customer information is handled across browsing, accounts, and orders.
privacy.intro: This page keeps privacy information structured and readable instead of dropping shoppers into a wall of legal text.

collection.title: What data we collect
collection.body: Information is generally collected when customers browse, create accounts, place orders, or contact support.

items.account: Account details such as name, email, phone number, and saved preferences.
items.orders: Order-related details such as delivery addresses, purchased items, and transaction context.
items.support: Messages, follow-up details, and support context provided by the customer.

usage.title: How data is used
usage.body: Collected information helps operate the storefront, support customers, and improve the experience over time.

items.fulfillment: To process orders, delivery steps, returns, and service follow-up.
items.improvement: To understand storefront usage and improve performance and experience.
items.marketing: To share relevant marketing updates where customers have opted into that communication.

sharing.title: Sharing, retention, and controls
sharing.body: Data may be shared only where operationally necessary, contractually appropriate, or legally required.

items.providers: Operational service providers may support payments, delivery, analytics, or communications.
items.compliance: Information may be disclosed where required for legal or compliance reasons.
items.retention: Records may be retained for operational, security, and legal purposes for an appropriate period.

cookies.title: Cookies and security
cookies.body: Cookies and related tools help keep the storefront usable, relevant, and more secure.

items.preferences: Preference cookies can remember language, cart context, and browsing continuity.
items.analytics: Analytics tools can help measure performance and identify friction points.
items.security: Security practices help protect accounts, sessions, and checkout-related activity.

cta.title: Need a privacy-related follow-up?
cta.description: Use the contact page if you need clarification about how your information is handled.

terms.title: Terms of Service
terms.subtitle: A structured terms page covering use of the storefront, accounts, pricing, orders, and limitations in plain language.
terms.intro: The terms page is organized to make the main rules and expectations easier to scan before or after purchase.

siteUse.title: Site use and content
siteUse.body: Using the storefront means accepting reasonable rules around access, information accuracy, and content use.

items.accuracy: Content is presented carefully, but availability and details can still change over time.
items.access: Access may be updated, limited, or interrupted when maintenance or operational issues require it.
items.content: Brand, product, and storefront content should not be reused in misleading ways.

accounts.title: Accounts and orders
accounts.body: Customers are responsible for keeping their account details accurate and their login credentials secure.

items.credentials: Account credentials should be protected and kept current.
items.orderDetails: Order details should be reviewed before confirmation.
items.availability: Order acceptance can still depend on stock, review, and operational feasibility.

pricing.title: Pricing and payments
pricing.body: Pricing, promotions, and payment details are presented as clearly as possible during the purchase journey.

items.prices: Displayed prices and discounts can change when catalog updates occur.
items.payments: Final payment handling depends on the methods currently offered during checkout.
items.promotions: Offers and promotional conditions may be limited by timing, stock, or other stated rules.

returns.title: Order changes, cancellations, and liability
returns.body: Order changes and cancellations depend on processing stage, operational feasibility, and the agreed payment path.

items.review: Confirmed orders may move into fulfillment quickly after approval.
items.refunds: Some changes or cancellations may not be possible once processing has started.
items.liability: Operational limits and reasonable service boundaries still apply to storefront use.

cta.title: Need clarification on the terms?
cta.description: Reach out through the contact page if you need help understanding a policy-related point.

cookies.title: Cookie Policy
cookies.subtitle: A clear overview of how cookies support storefront functionality, analytics, personalization, and security.
cookies.intro: This page explains cookies in a practical way so shoppers know what they do and how changing cookie behavior can affect the experience.

essential.title: Essential cookies
essential.body: Some cookies are closely tied to core storefront functionality and security.

items.session: Session continuity helps keep browsing and sign-in states stable.
items.cart: Cart-related cookies help preserve items while customers continue shopping.
items.security: Security cookies can help identify suspicious activity and protect sessions.

analytics.title: Analytics and personalization
analytics.body: Other cookies may help understand performance and improve the relevance of the storefront experience.

items.performance: Analytics can show where pages perform well or where customers struggle.
items.preferences: Preference cookies can remember choices such as language or browsing context.
items.relevance: Personalization tools can support a more relevant shopping journey over time.

manage.title: Managing cookies
manage.body: Customers can often manage cookies through browser settings, though some changes may affect the shopping experience.

items.browser: Browser controls can be used to delete or block certain cookies.
items.tradeoff: Blocking cookies may reduce convenience or break parts of the storefront flow.
items.support: If you are unsure what to change, contact support for guidance.

cta.title: Want the broader privacy picture?
cta.description: Read the privacy page for a fuller explanation of data handling and customer controls.

accessibility.title: Accessibility
accessibility.subtitle: An accessibility statement that explains the storefront’s intent, current practices, and room for ongoing improvement.
accessibility.intro: The page is written to stay credible: it explains the accessibility direction honestly without overstating compliance claims.

commitment.title: Accessibility commitment
commitment.description: The storefront aims to keep navigation, structure, and content understandable across devices and languages.

structure.title: Semantic structure
structure.description: Readable page structure, clear sections, and practical interaction patterns help support broader access.

feedback.title: Feedback welcomed
feedback.description: Customers should have a visible way to report accessibility barriers and ask for help.

approach.title: Our current approach
approach.body: Accessibility work is treated as an ongoing product-quality concern rather than a one-time badge.

items.navigation: Navigation and page flow should remain understandable across major storefront routes.
items.content: Headings, content grouping, and visible labels should stay consistent and readable.
items.forms: Interactive elements and forms should be reviewed with accessibility in mind.

limitations.title: Known limitations and iteration
limitations.body: Some limitations may still exist, especially where third-party content or evolving storefront features are involved.

items.thirdParty: Third-party integrations can introduce accessibility gaps outside the core page shell.
items.media: Some media or complex UI states may need further refinement over time.
items.iteration: The team can continue improving the experience as issues are identified.

cta.title: Need to report an accessibility issue?
cta.description: Use the contact route and explain the barrier you found so the team can review it properly.
